note: M11c (F5/D2b, R1 gate) — D2 bake-off RESOLVED: full-unroll (b) CHOSEN. The full-unroll renderer writes the hex.vec2 register framebuffer (0x06, fj 1.5.1) so the per-pixel deposit is eliminated. THE load-bearing finding: the heavy per-pixel body must live in ONE shared stl.fcall leaf, NOT inlined per pixel — inlining (~1100 ops/px) makes the flat assemble super-linear (the flipjump assembler wflip-resolution pass is ~cubic) and blows the 160x100 frame to 541-623s (2x the 300s ceiling, the R-2 wall). Shared-leaf shell (fcall + fixed-addr xor_zero ~40 ops/px) cuts unrolled code ~25x -> 20.5s assemble. src/fj/frame_render.fj (frame.setup_col + frame.pixel + frame.leaf_body), present.update_screen_reg (0x06), reference_model.render_unroll_frame, build.build_unroll_frame, scripts/m11c_evidence.py. M12-representative: the leaf reads per-column base/light/step from registers.

frame.scale: 160x100 (16000 px, full WIDTH-scale, synthetic full-width textured frame)

renderer: full-unroll (b), shared stl.fcall leaf + hex.vec2 register framebuffer (0x06)
leaf_micro_opts: kept-zero v3 (xor/xor_zero, no per-pixel hex.zero); single-nibble mask
gate: R1 PASSED — assemble 20.5s <= 300s ceiling; (b) ops/frame <= (a) by construction; flat, golden bit-exact vs H5. Owner-approved 2026-06-22.
